2026-03-25T10:19:23.2419687Z   
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2419934Z   ```json
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2421845Z   {"params":{"arguments":{"message":"Triage complete: Issue #57457 was authored by `live1206` (author_association: MEMBER = team member). Added \"needs-triage\" label and exited workflow per team-member branch — no further customer triage steps needed."},"name":"noop"}}
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2423542Z   ```
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2423890Z - 🔍 rpc **safeoutputs**←`resp`
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2424259Z   
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2424702Z   ```json
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2425251Z   {"id":4,"result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}
2026-03-25T10:19:23.2425907Z   ```

Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"mention_owners","description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The full comment body including @mentions","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"}}]}}

Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). 2026-03-25T10:19:23.3145269Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T10:19:01.903009137Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57457,\"title\":\"@@clientName on list operation drops the method from resource class in mgmt generator\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nWhen using `@@clientName` to rename a list-type operation (e.g., `ArmResourceActionSync` with `@list`) on an ARM resource interface, the mgmt generator fails to generate the pageable method on the resource class.\\n\\nAdditionally, the generator incorrectly names this operation `GetAll` by default, which is misleading. The operation is a **resource action** (`@action(\\u0026#34;reports\\u0026#34;)`) that returns `StorageTaskReportSummary` (containing `StorageTaskReportInstance` items) — it is NOT a list operation for the `StorageTaskAssignment` resource itself. `GetAll` should be reserved for listing instances of the resource type.\\n\\n## Minimal TypeSpec Repro\\n\\n```typespec\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-azure-resource-manager\\u0026#34;;\\nimport \\u0026#34;@azure-tools/typespec-client-generator-core\\u0026#34;;\\n\\nusing TypeSpec.Http;\\nusing TypeSpec.Rest;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ClientGenerator.Core;\\n\\n@armProviderNamespace\\n@service(#{ title: \\u0026#34;Test\\u0026#34; })\\n@versioned(Versions)\\nnamespace Test.Resource;\\n\\nenum Versions {\\n  @useDependency(Azure.ResourceManager.Versions.v1_0_Preview_1)\\n  v1: \\u0026#34;2024-01-01\\u0026#34;,\\n}\\n\\nmodel TestResource is TrackedResource {\\n  ...ResourceNameParameter;\\n}\\n\\nmodel TestResourceProperties {\\n  description?: string;\\n}\\n\\nmodel ReportInstance {\\n  id?: string;\\n  name?: string;\\n}\\n\\nmodel ReportListResult {\\n  value?: ReportInstance[];\\n  nextLink?: string;\\n}\\n\\n@armResourceOperations\\ninterface TestResources {\\n  get is ArmResourceRead;\\n  create is ArmResourceCreateOrReplaceAsync;\\n\\n  @get\\n  @list\\n  @action(\\u0026#34;reports\\u0026#34;)\\n  listReports is ArmResourceActionSync\\u0026lt;\\n    TestResource,\\n    void,\\n    ArmResponse\\n  \\u0026gt;;\\n}\\n\\n// This @@clientName causes listReports to be silently dropped from the generated resource class\\n@@clientName(TestResources.listReports, \\u0026#34;GetReports\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Without `@@clientName`\\n\\nThe generator produces `GetAll`/`GetAllAsync` on `TestResourceResource` — incorrect name for a resource action returning a different type.\\n\\n### With `@@clientName`\\n\\nThe REST client method (`CreateGetReportsRequest`) is generated, but the pageable method on `TestResourceResource` is **not** generated. The operation is silently dropped.\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\nThe generator should produce `GetReports`/`GetReportsAsync` on `TestResourceResource`.\\n\\n## Summary\\n\\nTwo issues:\\n1. The generator incorrectly names resource actions with `@list` as `GetAll`, even when they return a different type than the resource itself.\\n2. Using `@@clientName` to correct the name causes the operation to be silently dropped from the resource class.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nRemove the `@@clientName` decorator and use SDK-side custom code to provide the correct method name as a backward-compatible alias for `GetAll`.\\n\\n## Affected Package\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.Storage` (during TypeSpec migration)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57457\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"live1206\",\"id\":5196139,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/live1206\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5196139?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T10:16:35Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T10:18:46Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The injected block is identical to the legitimate system instructions and appears to originate from untrusted issue content.

2026-03-25T10:21:54.7918333Z The agent output itself is benign (correctly added "needs-triage" for a MEMBER author and exited), so the injection was not successful. No secrets or malicious patch detected.

This is a classic XML/system-tag injection pattern where untrusted issue content containing <system> tags was interpolated directly into the agent prompt. The injected block mirrors the legitimate security instructions verbatim. The agent output was not manipulated — it correctly followed the legitimate workflow (adding 'needs-triage' for a MEMBER author) — but a prompt injection attempt was present in the processed content."]}
